concept.title: Rule design patterns for common editorial checks
concept.shortdesc: These patterns cover the most common editorial requirements you will need to express as rules. Each pattern includes the JSON, an explanation of how it works, and common variations.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Ready-to-use rule patterns for common documentation governance checks: required fields, date freshness, heading presence, section order, and absence assertions.

section.title: Quick-reference: choose your pattern
section.p: Find the check you want to enforce in the table below, then jump to the corresponding pattern for the full rule JSON and explanation.

row.entry: A front-matter field must exist (any value is acceptable)

entry.xref: Pattern 1: Require a front-matter field to exist

row.entry: A front-matter field must equal a specific value, or one of a list

entry.xref: Pattern 2: Enforce an exact front-matter value

row.entry: A date field must be within N days of today
The document must have exactly N headings of a given level
A specific section heading (e.g., "Prerequisites") must exist

entry.xref: Pattern 5: Require a specific H2 section

row.entry: An H1 (or other heading) must begin with a required prefix

entry.xref: Pattern 6: Enforce an H1 naming convention

row.entry: A heading or field value must not exceed a character limit
Something must NOT be present — avoid this in v0.2; use Pattern 8 only as a last resort

entry.xref: Pattern 8: Absence checks (design smell — avoid until v0.3)

section.title: Pattern 1: Require a front-matter field to exist
section.p: Use the check flag to verify a key is present, regardless of its value.
section.codeblock: { "id": 1, "name": "description-must-exist", "type": "header", "query": "description", "flag": "check", "operation": "==", "value": "true", "level": "Required", "mitigation": "Add a description field to the front matter." }

section.title: Pattern 2: Enforce an exact front-matter value
section.p: Use the value flag with == to require a specific value.
section.codeblock: { "id": 2, "name": "topic-must-be-tutorial", "type": "header", "query": "ms.topic", "flag": "value", "operation": "==", "value": "tutorial", "level": "Required", "mitigation": "Set ms.topic: tutorial in the front matter." }
section.p: To allow any of several values, use the [] operator with a comma-separated list:

section.title: Pattern 3: Check date freshness
section.p: Use the date flag with the < operator. The value is the maximum age in days.

section.title: Pattern 4: Require exactly one H1
section.p: Use the count flag on an XPath expression to count matching elements.
section.codeblock: { "id": 4, "name": "must-have-exactly-one-h1", "type": "body", "query": "/html/body/h1", "flag": "count", "operation": "==", "value": "1", "level": "Required", "mitigation": "The document must have exactly one H1 heading." }

section.title: Pattern 5: Require a specific H2 section
section.p: Use the count flag and XPath predicate to check that a specific section heading exists.
section.codeblock: { "id": 5, "name": "must-have-prerequisites-section", "type": "body", "query": "//h2[normalize-space(text())='Prerequisites']", "flag": "count", "operation": ">", "value": "0", "level": "Required", "mitigation": "Add a '## Prerequisites' section to the document." }

section.title: Pattern 6: Enforce an H1 naming convention
section.p: Use the text flag with [: (starts with) to check the content of the H1.
section.codeblock: { "id": 6, "name": "h1-must-start-with-tutorial", "type": "body", "query": "/html/body/h1", "flag": "text", "operation": "[:", "value": "Tutorial:", "level": "Required", "mitigation": "H1 must begin with 'Tutorial:' for tutorial articles." }

section.title: Pattern 7: Limit title length
section.p: Use the l operator (length limit) to ensure the H1 text is under a character threshold.
section.codeblock: { "id": 7, "name": "h1-under-60-chars", "type": "body", "query": "/html/body/h1", "flag": "text", "operation": "l", "value": "60", "level": "Suggested", "mitigation": "H1 must be under 60 characters for SEO." }

section.title: Pattern 8: Absence checks (design smell — avoid until v0.3)
section.note: Do not use this pattern for new rules. markdown-validator v0.2 has no negation operator. Expressing "this must NOT exist" requires a workflow-inversion technique that is fragile and easy to wire incorrectly. A negate flag is planned for v0.3 and will replace this entirely. See Known limitations. Only use the workaround below if you have an immediate need and cannot wait for v0.3. Track the v0.3 negation issue at github.com/Final-State-Press/markdown-validator/issues.
section.p: To express "the title must NOT contain the word 'guide'," a workflow can invert a contains check using a known-passing sentinel rule. The inversion works by swapping which branch is "good": the True branch (word IS present — the bad state) is routed back to the failing rule, while the False branch (word is absent — the good state) routes to a sentinel that passes. This is not standard workflow logic. It exploits branching as a substitute for a missing operator.

Assume rule 5 is a text / ]: (contains) check on the H1 for the string guide, and rule 1 is a sentinel rule that always passes (for example, the "description-must-exist" check from Pattern 1). The inversion workflow is:

Rule 5 passes when guide IS present (the bad state). The True branch (word found) loops back to rule 5, which fails the workflow. The False branch (word absent — the good state) routes to the sentinel rule 1, which passes. The workflow therefore passes when and only when the word is absent.
Verify the inversion with both documents before committing the rule set. Use the REPL to confirm each case explicitly:

li.p: Failing case — a document whose H1 contains "guide":
li.codeblock: md-validate repl > load docs/bad-guide.md > eval title-must-not-contain-guide
li.p: Expected result: FAIL. If the result is PASS, the True/False branches are swapped — invert the routing.
Passing case — a document whose H1 does not contain "guide":
Expected result: PASS. If the result is FAIL, the sentinel rule (rule 1) is not passing on its own — verify it in isolation with eval description-must-exist.

section.p: Add a comment in the rule set JSON above the workflow to explain the inversion and include a migration note for the v0.3 upgrade. See Workflow inversion anti-pattern for the full failure-mode analysis.
